D3 Chemistry L5 Evaluate Reference

F-Centres — Trapped Electron in an Anion Vacancy

irradiate NaCl or KCl with X-rays or fast neutrons and a chloride ion leaves; an electron drops into the empty hole and absorbs in the visible — the crystal turns yellow/violet

A colour centre (Farbenzentrum, F-centre) is a point defect: a missing halide ion (anion vacancy) whose negative charge is replaced by a trapped electron. The electron's allowed energy levels in the cavity have spacings in the visible — so the crystal absorbs a specific colour and the complement shines through. NaCl absorbs 460 nm → yellow-orange tint. KCl absorbs 560 nm → magenta tint. Different host lattice → different colour.

Pick a host crystal. The schematic shows the trapped electron in the vacancy; the absorption band and resulting tint follow.
